nodes.name: Arabian eagle-owl
nodes.summary: Arabian eagle-owl（Arabian eagle-owl，学名 Bubo milesi），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，常见栖息地包括峡谷峭壁。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Accessed on 23 June 2024. | status2 = CITES_A2 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | taxon = Bubo milesi | authority = Sharpe, 1886 | synonyms = }} The Arabian eagle-owl (Bubo milesi) is a true owl, of the family Strigidae, endemic to areas of the Arabian Peninsula, known from southwestern Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Oman and the United Arab Emirates. Primarily, the species is known from the coastal areas and slightly inland of the peninsula, seemingly avoiding the most barren of the region's desert interior. It nests in wadis, cliffs, on canyon walls, large palms and other trees
